---
title: "Nature-based Solutions and Political Risk: A Cross-Country Analysis"
author: ""
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: hide
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 7
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
# Set working directory to project root (one level above analysis/)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath(file.path(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE), "..")))
```

## Overview

This document examines how country-level **total Nature-based Solutions (NbS)
mitigation potential** correlates with **World Governance Indicators (WGI)** and
whether political risk shapes **market-based NbS implementation**. Three datasets
are integrated:

1. **Naturebase v3** (2025) — biophysical NbS potential in tCO₂e/yr for 20
   pathways across 216 countries (`total_ncs_tco2e`)
2. **World Bank WGI 2024** — six governance dimensions across 216 countries
3. **Karnik et al. 2024** (*Zenodo* doi:10.5281/zenodo.11459391) — 575
   market-based carbon offset projects (AD, IFM, ARR) in 55 countries

**Political risk dimensions (WGI):**

| Code      | Dimension                                      |
|-----------|------------------------------------------------|
| CC.EST    | Control of Corruption                          |
| GE.EST    | Government Effectiveness                       |
| PV.EST    | Political Stability & Absence of Violence      |
| RL.EST    | Rule of Law                                    |
| RQ.EST    | Regulatory Quality                             |
| VA.EST    | Voice & Accountability                         |

> **Additional source — Fragile States Index (FSI):** Download the latest annual
> CSV from <https://fragilestatesindex.org/> and place it in
> `data/fsi/fsi_latest.csv` to activate Section 8. The FSI provides 12
> sub-indicators (Security Apparatus, State Legitimacy, Factionalized Elites,
> etc.) that complement the broader WGI governance dimensions.

---

## Paper Write-up

### Methods

**Data sources.** We integrated three country-level datasets. First, we used
biophysical NbS mitigation potential estimates from **Naturebase v3** (The Nature
Conservancy, 2025), which provides annual sequestration and avoided-emission
potential in tCO₂e yr⁻¹ across 20 pathways spanning cropland, forest, grassland
and wetland ecosystems for 216 countries. We focused on the total NCS potential
per country (`total_ncs_tco2e`), the sum across all 20 pathways, as well as
biome-level totals (cropland, forest, grassland, wetland) and activity-type totals
(protection, management, restoration).

Second, we measured political risk using the **World Governance Indicators (WGI)**
2024 release (World Bank). We used the Political Stability and Absence of
Violence/Terrorism dimension (PV.EST; scale −2.5 to +2.5) as our primary measure
of political risk, with lower scores indicating higher instability and conflict
exposure. We also ran sensitivity analyses using all six WGI dimensions
(Voice & Accountability, Government Effectiveness, Regulatory Quality, Rule of
Law, and Control of Corruption).

Third, we constructed two complementary measures of NbS project implementation.
*Market-based implementation* was measured using the global database of
**nature-based carbon offset project boundaries** (Karnik et al., 2024;
doi:10.5281/zenodo.11459391), which catalogues 575 georeferenced projects across
55 countries from six carbon registries (Verra, *n* = 294; Climate Action Reserve,
*n* = 134; American Carbon Registry, *n* = 96; EcoRegistry, *n* = 33; Gold
Standard, *n* = 17; BioCarbon Registry, *n* = 1), spanning three project types:
Improved Forest Management (IFM, *n* = 252), Afforestation/Reforestation/
Revegetation (ARR, *n* = 190), and Avoided Deforestation (AD, *n* = 133).
*Community-based implementation* was measured using the **UNDP Equator Initiative
Solutions Database**, scraped via the WordPress REST API (July 2025), yielding
2,351 community NbS initiatives across 140 countries with geolocation data.

**Analytical approach.** We matched NbS potential data with WGI scores for 189
countries. To measure **implementation efficiency** — the degree to which countries
translate biophysical carbon opportunity into actual projects — we computed total
projects (market + community) per MtCO₂e yr⁻¹ of NbS potential for each country.
Both NbS potential and the efficiency ratio were log₁₀-transformed prior to
analysis to account for right-skewed distributions. We estimated bivariate
relationships using Spearman rank correlations (robust to non-normality) and
ordinary least-squares regressions controlling for income group (World Bank
classification). All analyses were conducted in R 4.6.0.

To examine how political risk constrains the *accessible* NbS portfolio under
different risk tolerances, we constructed **cumulative potential curves** for each
NbS type. Countries were sorted from most to least politically stable (descending
PV.EST) and cumulative NbS potential was computed as each successive country was
added. The resulting curves — expressed as a percentage of global type-level total
— show the share of potential accessible if an investor or policymaker limits
engagement to countries above a given stability threshold. We report this
separately for four biome types (cropland, forest, grassland, wetland) and three
activity types (protection, management, restoration), with risk zones defined at
PV.EST thresholds of −1 (fragile), 0 (average), and +1 (stable).

---

### Figure Captions

**Figure 1.** Political stability and NbS biophysical potential and implementation
efficiency across 189 countries. **(A)** Country-level total NbS mitigation
potential (Naturebase v3, 2025) as a function of political stability (WGI
Political Stability & Absence of Violence, 2024). Countries are coloured by World
Bank income group. The black line shows an OLS regression fit with 95% confidence
interval (Spearman *ρ* = −0.47, *p* < 0.001). High-potential countries are
disproportionately concentrated among lower-income, less stable nations.
**(B)** NbS implementation efficiency — total projects (market-based carbon offset
projects, Karnik et al. 2024, *n* = 575; and community-based NbS initiatives,
Equator Initiative 2024, *n* = 1,677 with location data) per MtCO₂e yr⁻¹ of
biophysical potential — as a function of political stability. Countries with high
NbS potential but zero recorded projects are labelled. The positive slope indicates
that more politically stable countries implement more projects relative to their
available carbon opportunity, despite holding less absolute potential.

```{r fig1-display, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="", out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("../analysis/figures/fig_paper_main.png")
```

---

**Figure 2.** Cumulative NbS mitigation potential accessible as a function of
political risk tolerance. The x-axis represents a minimum political stability
threshold (WGI PV.EST, 2024): reading left-to-right, progressively higher-risk
countries are included. The y-axis shows the cumulative potential of each NbS type
as a percentage of its global total. Coloured lines represent four **biome types**
(Forest, Grassland, Cropland, Wetland; Naturebase v3, 2025). Grey lines represent
three **activity types** (Protection: solid; Management: long-dash; Restoration:
dotted). Shaded risk zones correspond to Stable (PV > 1), Moderate (0 < PV ≤ 1),
High Risk (−1 < PV ≤ 0), and Fragile (PV < −1) country classifications. Steeper
curves indicate greater concentration of potential in high-risk countries.

```{r fig2-display, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="", out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("../analysis/figures/fig_threshold_nbs_type.png")
```

---

### Interpretation

**The carbon opportunity is disproportionately located in high-risk countries.**
Figure 1A shows a significant negative relationship between NbS biophysical
potential and political stability (Spearman *ρ* = −0.47, *p* < 0.001): countries
with the largest total carbon sequestration and avoided-emission potential —
principally in the Congo Basin, Amazon, and Southeast Asia — tend to have
substantially lower political stability scores. This is not coincidental; tropical
forest ecosystems, which harbour the majority of global NbS potential, are
concentrated in regions that are also among the most politically fragile.

**Despite this, stable countries implement more efficiently.** Figure 1B reveals
the implementation gap: when project counts are normalised by biophysical potential
(projects per MtCO₂e yr⁻¹), the relationship with political stability reverses to
positive. High-stability countries implement substantially more projects per unit of
available carbon opportunity than their higher-risk counterparts. This divergence —
potential inversely related to stability, but efficiency positively related — is the
core tension motivating this paper: the places with the most to offer are
systematically underserved by NbS investment.

**The risk barrier is sharpest for protection and forest-type NbS.** Figure 2
disaggregates this result by NbS type. Protection-type activities (solid grey line)
show the steepest cumulative curve, meaning that their potential is most
concentrated in fragile and high-risk countries: less than 25% of global protection
potential lies in countries with above-average stability (PV > 0). Forest NbS
(dark green) follows a similar pattern, with the bulk of potential entering the
cumulative total only as the fragile zone is included. By contrast, Cropland
(orange) and Management-type activities (long-dashed grey) show gentler curves,
accumulating more potential in moderate- and low-risk countries, suggesting
relatively greater opportunities for implementation without entering fragile
contexts.

**Practical implications.** For investors and policy-makers operating with strict
risk constraints — accepting only countries with PV > 0 — the accessible global NbS
portfolio is substantially truncated, and predominantly cropland and management
activities. Accessing the full scale of forest protection and wetland conservation
potential requires either accepting higher political risk or deploying risk
mitigation instruments (political risk insurance, sovereign guarantees, blended
finance) specifically targeted at fragile and conflict-affected states.

---

## 1. Package Setup

```{r packages}
# Install any missing packages before running:
# install.packages(c("tidyverse", "readxl", "ggcorrplot", "ggrepel",
#                    "countrycode", "patchwork", "broom", "scales", "here"))

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)         # Read local WGI Excel files
library(ggcorrplot)     # Correlation heatmaps
library(ggrepel)        # Non-overlapping labels on scatter plots
library(countrycode)    # Country code harmonisation
library(patchwork)      # Combine ggplots
library(broom)          # Tidy model output
library(scales)
library(here)
```

---

## 2. Load Naturebase Data

```{r load-nbs}
nbs_raw <- read_csv(here("data/all_pathways/all_pathways_tco2e_adm0.csv"))

# ── Pathway display labels ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pathway_labels <- c(
  # Individual pathways
  crp_cba_tco2e  = "Cropland Agroforestry",
  crp_rec_tco2e  = "Reduced Emissions (Crops)",
  crp_scc_tco2e  = "Soil Carbon (Crops)",
  for_afc_tco2e  = "Avoided Forest Conversion",
  for_csf_tco2e  = "Climate-Smart Forestry",
  for_ref_tco2e  = "Forest Reforestation",
  for_rwf_tco2e  = "Reduced Woodland Fire (Forest)",
  grs_agc_tco2e  = "Agroforestry (Grassland)",
  grs_asc_tco2e  = "Avoided Savanna Conversion",
  grs_grr_tco2e  = "Grassland Restoration",
  grs_reg_tco2e  = "Reforestation (Grassland)",
  grs_rwf_tco2e  = "Reduced Grassland Fire",
  grs_sba_tco2e  = "Savanna Burning Management",
  grs_scg_tco2e  = "Soil Carbon (Grassland)",
  grs_sfm_tco2e  = "Sustainable Forest Mgmt",
  wet_apc_tco2e  = "Avoided Peatland Conversion",
  wet_awc_tco2e  = "Avoided Wetland Conversion",
  wet_cwr_tco2e  = "Coastal Wetland Restoration",
  wet_ipm_tco2e  = "Improved Peatland Mgmt",
  wet_per_tco2e  = "Peatland Restoration",
  # Biome aggregates
  total_crop_tco2e    = "Total: Cropland",
  total_forest_tco2e  = "Total: Forest",
  total_grass_tco2e   = "Total: Grassland",
  total_wet_tco2e     = "Total: Wetland",
  total_peat_tco2e    = "Total: Peatland",
  # Activity-type aggregates
  total_protect_tco2e = "Total: Protection",
  total_manage_tco2e  = "Total: Management",
  total_restore_tco2e = "Total: Restoration",
  total_ncs_tco2e     = "Total NCS"
)

nbs_individual <- names(pathway_labels)[1:20]
nbs_biome      <- names(pathway_labels)[21:25]
nbs_activity   <- names(pathway_labels)[26:29]
nbs_grand      <- "total_ncs_tco2e"

cat("Countries in Naturebase:", nrow(nbs_raw), "\n")
glimpse(nbs_raw)
```

---

## 3. Load World Governance Indicators (Local Excel)

Data are read from the locally downloaded WGI 2025 dataset
(`data/wgidataset_with_sourcedata-2025.xlsx`). The most recent year (2024)
is used for each country.

```{r load-wgi}
# Short alias → display label (used for plot titles, table headers)
wgi_codes <- c(
  CC.EST = "Control of Corruption",
  GE.EST = "Government Effectiveness",
  PV.EST = "Political Stability & No Violence",
  RL.EST = "Rule of Law",
  RQ.EST = "Regulatory Quality",
  VA.EST = "Voice & Accountability"
)

# Sheet name in the Excel file for each dimension
wgi_sheet_map <- c(
  CC.EST = "cc",
  GE.EST = "ge",
  PV.EST = "pv",
  RL.EST = "rl",
  RQ.EST = "rq",
  VA.EST = "va"
)

wgi_excel_path <- here("data", "wgidataset_with_sourcedata-2025.xlsx")

# Read each sheet and extract the governance estimate for the most recent year
read_wgi_sheet <- function(dim_code) {
  read_excel(wgi_excel_path, sheet = wgi_sheet_map[dim_code]) %>%
    rename(
      wb_code      = `Economy (code)`,
      country      = `Economy (name)`,
      region       = Region,
      income_level = `Income classification`,
      year         = Year,
      estimate     = `Governance estimate (approx. -2.5 to +2.5)`
    ) %>%
    select(wb_code, country, region, income_level, year, estimate) %>%
    filter(!is.na(estimate)) %>%
    group_by(wb_code) %>%
    slice_max(year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(!!dim_code := estimate)
}

wgi_sheets <- lapply(names(wgi_sheet_map), read_wgi_sheet)

# Wide format: one row per country, one column per WGI dimension
wgi_wide <- wgi_sheets[[1]] %>%
  select(wb_code, country, region, income_level, CC.EST)
for (i in 2:length(wgi_sheets)) {
  dim_col <- names(wgi_sheet_map)[i]
  wgi_wide <- left_join(
    wgi_wide,
    wgi_sheets[[i]] %>% select(wb_code, !!sym(dim_col)),
    by = "wb_code"
  )
}

# Convert World Bank country codes → ISO3
# countrycode handles most; manual overrides for legacy WB codes
wgi <- wgi_wide %>%
  mutate(
    iso3c = countrycode(wb_code, origin = "wb", destination = "iso3c",
                        warn = FALSE),
    iso3c = case_when(
      wb_code == "ADO" ~ "AND",   # Andorra
      wb_code == "ZAR" ~ "COD",   # DR Congo
      wb_code == "ROM" ~ "ROU",   # Romania
      wb_code == "TMP" ~ "TLS",   # Timor-Leste
      wb_code == "PSE" ~ "PSE",   # Palestine
      !is.na(iso3c)   ~ iso3c,
      TRUE             ~ wb_code
    ),
    income_level = factor(income_level,
      levels = c("Low income", "Lower middle income",
                 "Upper middle income", "High income"))
  )

cat("WGI records loaded:", nrow(wgi), "\n")
```

---

## 4. Merge NbS + Governance Data

```{r merge}
df <- nbs_raw %>%
  rename(iso3c = adm0_id) %>%
  left_join(wgi, by = "iso3c") %>%
  filter(!is.na(CC.EST))  # require at least one WGI estimate

cat("Countries matched:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Countries without WGI (dropped):", nrow(nbs_raw) - nrow(df), "\n")

# Compute per-hectare NCS density for size-normalised comparisons
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(c(nbs_individual, nbs_biome, nbs_activity, nbs_grand)),
      ~ . / adm0_ha,
      .names = "{.col}_dens"   # tCO2e / ha_country / yr
    )
  )
```

---

## 5. Descriptive Statistics

```{r descriptives}
desc <- df %>%
  select(total_ncs_tco2e, all_of(names(wgi_codes))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(
    n      = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean   = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min    = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(value, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label = coalesce(pathway_labels[name], wgi_codes[name], name)
  ) %>%
  relocate(label, .after = name)

knitr::kable(desc, digits = 2,
  caption = "Descriptive statistics — Total NCS potential (tCO₂e/yr) and WGI scores")
```

---

## 6. Total NCS × WGI Spearman Correlations

Spearman rank correlations between **log1p(Total NCS tCO₂e/yr)** and each
WGI governance dimension. p-values are from a two-sided test with a
continuity correction.

```{r cor-total-ncs}
wgi_vars <- names(wgi_codes)

# Compute Spearman rho + p-value for each WGI dim vs total NCS
cor_ncs <- map_dfr(wgi_vars, function(wv) {
  tmp <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(total_ncs_tco2e), total_ncs_tco2e > 0, !is.na(.data[[wv]])) %>%
    mutate(log_ncs = log1p(total_ncs_tco2e))
  ct  <- cor.test(tmp$log_ncs, tmp[[wv]], method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  tibble(
    wgi_var   = wv,
    wgi_label = wgi_codes[wv],
    rho       = ct$estimate,
    p_value   = ct$p.value,
    n         = nrow(tmp),
    sig       = case_when(
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ ""
    )
  )
})

cor_ncs %>%
  select(wgi_label, rho, p_value, n, sig) %>%
  mutate(across(c(rho, p_value), ~ round(.x, 4))) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("WGI Dimension", "Spearman ρ", "p-value", "N", "Sig."),
    caption   = "Spearman correlations: log1p(Total NCS tCO₂e/yr) × WGI (2024)"
  )
```

```{r cor-bar, fig.height=4, fig.width=7}
cor_ncs %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rho, y = reorder(wgi_label, rho),
             fill = rho > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(rho, 3), sig),
                x     = rho + ifelse(rho >= 0, 0.01, -0.01)),
            hjust = ifelse(cor_ncs$rho >= 0, 0, 1), size = 3.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#1a9850", "FALSE" = "#d73027")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.4, 0.6), expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Spearman ρ: Total NCS Potential × WGI Governance Dimensions",
    subtitle = "* p<0.05  ** p<0.01  *** p<0.001  |  log1p(tCO₂e/yr), N≈200 countries",
    x = "Spearman ρ", y = NULL
  )
```

---

## 7. Scatter Plots: Total NCS vs. Each WGI Dimension

Each panel shows **log₁₀(Total NCS tCO₂e/yr)** against one WGI dimension.
Countries in the top 10% of NCS potential or bottom 10% of governance are
labelled. The OLS trend line (with 95% CI) is shown in black.

```{r scatter-all-wgi, fig.height=9, fig.width=11}
label_thresh <- quantile(df$total_ncs_tco2e, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)

make_scatter <- function(wv) {
  risk_thresh <- quantile(df[[wv]], 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[wv]]), total_ncs_tco2e > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      log_ncs = log10(total_ncs_tco2e),
      label   = if_else(
        total_ncs_tco2e > label_thresh | .data[[wv]] < risk_thresh,
        adm0_name, NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[wv]], y = log_ncs)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = income_level), alpha = 0.70, size = 1.9) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
                linewidth = 0.7, fill = "grey80") +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 2.4, max.overlaps = 12,
                    segment.color = "grey55", segment.size = 0.3) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = NULL, na.value = "grey70",
                       guide = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
    labs(
      x     = wgi_codes[wv],
      y     = "log₁₀(Total NCS tCO₂e/yr)",
      title = wgi_codes[wv]
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.text     = element_text(size = 7),
          plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10))
}

plots <- map(wgi_vars, make_scatter)

wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 3, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

## 8. Fragile States Index (Optional)

Place the annual FSI CSV (`fsi_latest.csv`) in `data/fsi/` to run this section.
Expected columns: `Country`, `Total`, plus 12 sub-indicator columns
(C1–C3, S1–S2, E1–E3, P1–P3, X1, and/or their full names).

```{r fsi, eval=file.exists(here("data/fsi/fsi_latest.csv"))}
fsi_raw <- read_csv(here("data/fsi/fsi_latest.csv"))

# Harmonise country names → ISO3 via countrycode
fsi <- fsi_raw %>%
  mutate(
    iso3c = countrycode(Country, origin = "country.name", destination = "iso3c",
                        warn = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3c)) %>%
  rename(fsi_total = Total) %>%
  select(iso3c, fsi_total, any_of(c(
    "C1: Security Apparatus", "C2: Factionalized Elites", "C3: Group Grievance",
    "E1: Economy", "E2: Economic Inequality", "E3: Human Flight and Brain Drain",
    "P1: State Legitimacy", "P2: Public Services", "P3: Human Rights",
    "S1: Demographic Pressures", "S2: Refugees and IDPs", "X1: External Intervention"
  )))

df_fsi <- df %>%
  left_join(fsi, by = "iso3c")

fsi_vars <- names(fsi)[-1]

cor_fsi_mat <- df_fsi %>%
  select(all_of(c(nbs_biome, nbs_grand, fsi_vars))) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(c(nbs_biome, nbs_grand)), log1p)) %>%
  cor(method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

cor_fsi_sub <- cor_fsi_mat[c(nbs_biome, nbs_grand), fsi_vars]
rownames(cor_fsi_sub) <- pathway_labels[rownames(cor_fsi_sub)]

knitr::kable(round(cor_fsi_sub, 3),
             caption = "Spearman rho — NbS biome totals × FSI sub-indicators")
```

---

## 9. Detailed Scatter: Total NCS vs. Political Stability

High-resolution view of the strongest governance relationship, with all
countries labelled by income group and region.

```{r scatter-ps-detail, fig.height=7, fig.width=10}
# Build as named object for reuse in two-panel figure
panel_A <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(PV.EST), total_ncs_tco2e > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    log_ncs = log10(total_ncs_tco2e),
    label   = if_else(
      total_ncs_tco2e > quantile(total_ncs_tco2e, 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) |
      PV.EST < quantile(PV.EST, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PV.EST, y = log_ncs)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = income_level), alpha = 0.80, size = 2.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = label), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 25,
    segment.color = "grey55", segment.size = 0.3, box.padding = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60") +
  labs(
    x = "Political Stability & Absence of Violence (WGI 2024)",
    y = "Total NCS Potential (log\u2081\u2080 tCO\u2082e yr\u207b\u00b9)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

panel_A

dir.create(here("analysis/figures"), showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig1_ncs_potential.png"),
       panel_A, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig1_ncs_potential.pdf"),
       panel_A, width = 9, height = 7)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig1_ncs_potential.png (.pdf)\n")
```

---

## 10. Sub-group Analysis by Income Group

Stratified Spearman correlations between Total NCS and each WGI dimension,
computed separately for each World Bank income group.

```{r subgroup-income-cor}
income_cors <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_level)) %>%
  group_by(income_level) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    across(
      all_of(wgi_vars),
      ~ cor(log1p(total_ncs_tco2e), .x,
            method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
      .names = "{.col}"
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(wgi_vars), names_to = "wgi_var", values_to = "rho") %>%
  mutate(wgi_label = wgi_codes[wgi_var])

ggplot(income_cors,
       aes(x = rho, y = reorder(income_level, rho, FUN = mean),
           color = wgi_label)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = "WGI Dimension") +
  labs(
    x     = "Spearman rho (log1p Total NCS ~ WGI)",
    y     = NULL,
    title = "NCS–Governance Correlations by Income Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r subgroup-income-facet, fig.height=8, fig.width=11}
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_level), total_ncs_tco2e > 0) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(wgi_vars),
               names_to = "wgi_var", values_to = "wgi_val") %>%
  mutate(wgi_label = wgi_codes[wgi_var]) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wgi_val, y = log1p(total_ncs_tco2e), color = income_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(income_level ~ wgi_label,
             labeller = labeller(wgi_label = label_wrap_gen(12))) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu") +
  labs(
    x     = "WGI Score",
    y     = "Total NCS (log1p tCO₂e/yr)",
    title = "Total NCS ~ WGI, stratified by income group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7))
```

---

## 11. Sub-group Analysis by World Bank Region

```{r subgroup-region, fig.height=6, fig.width=12}
region_cors <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(region)) %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    across(
      all_of(wgi_vars),
      ~ cor(log1p(total_ncs_tco2e), .x,
            method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
      .names = "{.col}"
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(wgi_vars), names_to = "wgi_var", values_to = "rho") %>%
  mutate(wgi_label = wgi_codes[wgi_var])

ggplot(region_cors,
       aes(x = rho, y = reorder(region, rho, FUN = mean),
           fill = wgi_label)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.85), width = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "WGI Dimension") +
  labs(
    x     = "Spearman rho (log1p Total NCS ~ WGI)",
    y     = NULL,
    title = "Regional Variation in NCS–Governance Correlations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
```

---

## 12. OLS Regression: Total NCS ~ All WGI Dimensions

A single OLS model regresses log1p Total NCS on all six WGI dimensions
simultaneously. This estimates the partial association of each governance
dimension conditional on the others.

```{r ols-regression, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
ols <- lm(
  log1p(total_ncs_tco2e) ~
    CC.EST + GE.EST + PV.EST + RL.EST + RQ.EST + VA.EST,
  data = df
)

summary(ols)

tidy(ols, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = wgi_codes[term]) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate,
             xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high,
             y = reorder(term, estimate))) +
  geom_pointrange(size = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x        = "OLS Coefficient (95% CI)",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "OLS: log1p(Total NCS) ~ WGI",
    subtitle = paste0("Adj. R² = ",
                      round(summary(ols)$adj.r.squared, 3),
                      " | N = ", nobs(ols))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

### Per-biome regressions

```{r ols-per-biome}
biome_models <- map(
  set_names(c(nbs_biome, nbs_grand)),
  ~ lm(
      reformulate(wgi_vars, response = paste0("log1p(", .x, ")")),
      data = df
    ) %>%
    tidy(conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      outcome   = pathway_labels[.x],
      wgi_label = wgi_codes[term]
    )
) %>%
  bind_rows()

ggplot(biome_models,
       aes(x = estimate, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high,
           y = reorder(wgi_label, estimate),
           color = outcome)) +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(0.7), size = 0.55) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "NbS Outcome") +
  facet_wrap(~outcome, nrow = 2) +
  labs(
    x     = "OLS Coefficient (95% CI)",
    y     = NULL,
    title = "Per-biome OLS: log1p(NbS potential) ~ WGI"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
```

---

## 13. Market-based NbS: Karnik et al. (2024) Carbon Offset Projects

A global database of **575 nature-based carbon offset project boundaries** across
55 countries (Karnik et al., 2024, *Zenodo* doi:10.5281/zenodo.11459391).
Projects span three types: Avoided Deforestation (AD), Improved Forest Management
(IFM), and Afforestation / Reforestation / Revegetation (ARR). This represents
**market-based, implemented** NbS activity — a complement to Naturebase's
biophysical potential estimates.

```{r load-karnik}
karnik_raw <- read_csv(here("data/karnik2024/carbon_projects_database_index.csv"),
                       show_col_types = FALSE)

# Aggregate to country level
karnik_cty <- karnik_raw %>%
  mutate(
    iso3c = countrycode(`Country`, origin = "country.name",
                        destination = "iso3c", warn = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3c)) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    n_projects = n(),
    n_ad       = sum(`Project Type` == "AD",  na.rm = TRUE),
    n_ifm      = sum(`Project Type` == "IFM", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_arr      = sum(`Project Type` == "ARR", na.rm = TRUE),
    n_verra    = sum(`Registry Name` == "Verra", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat(sprintf("Karnik: %d countries with ≥1 project | %d projects total\n",
            nrow(karnik_cty), sum(karnik_cty$n_projects)))
```

### 13.1 Project Count by Country

```{r karnik-bar, fig.height=6}
karnik_cty %>%
  left_join(select(df, iso3c, country_name = adm0_name, region), by = "iso3c") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_projects)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 25) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_projects, y = reorder(country_name, n_projects),
             fill = region)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_projects), hjust = -0.2, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey70") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(title = "Top 25 Countries by Carbon Offset Project Count",
       subtitle = "Karnik et al. 2024 — 575 NbS projects across 55 countries",
       x = "Number of Projects", y = NULL, fill = "Region")
```

### 13.2 Join with WGI Data

```{r karnik-join}
df_k <- df %>%
  left_join(karnik_cty, by = "iso3c") %>%
  mutate(
    has_project  = !is.na(n_projects),
    n_projects   = replace_na(n_projects, 0),
    log_projects = log1p(n_projects)
  )

cat(sprintf("Countries with ≥1 project: %d / %d\n",
            sum(df_k$has_project), nrow(df_k)))
```

### 13.3 Correlations: Project Count × WGI

```{r karnik-cor}
cor_k <- map_dfr(wgi_vars, function(wv) {
  tmp <- df_k %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[wv]]))
  ct  <- cor.test(tmp$log_projects, tmp[[wv]],
                  method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  tibble(
    wgi_var   = wv,
    wgi_label = wgi_codes[wv],
    rho       = ct$estimate,
    p_value   = ct$p.value,
    sig       = case_when(p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
                          p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
                          p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
                          TRUE            ~ "")
  )
})

cor_k %>%
  mutate(across(c(rho, p_value), ~ round(.x, 4))) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("WGI Dim.", "Label", "Spearman ρ", "p-value", "Sig."),
    caption   = "Spearman ρ: log1p(N projects) × WGI (2024)"
  )
```

```{r karnik-cor-bar, fig.height=4, fig.width=7}
cor_k %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rho, y = reorder(wgi_label, rho), fill = rho > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(rho, 3), sig),
                x     = rho + ifelse(rho >= 0, 0.01, -0.01)),
            hjust = ifelse(cor_k$rho >= 0, 0, 1), size = 3.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#1a9850", "FALSE" = "#d73027")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 0.7), expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)) +
  labs(title = "Spearman ρ: Carbon Offset Projects × WGI",
       subtitle = "log1p(N projects per country); * p<0.05  ** p<0.01  *** p<0.001",
       x = "Spearman ρ", y = NULL)
```

### 13.4 Potential vs. Realised: NbS Gap Analysis

Countries with high biophysical potential but few market projects represent the
largest implementation gap. Color encodes income group; size encodes number of
market projects.

```{r karnik-gap, fig.height=6, fig.width=9}
# Build as named object for reuse in two-panel figure
panel_B <- df_k %>%
  filter(total_ncs_tco2e > 0, !is.na(PV.EST)) %>%
  mutate(
    log_ncs = log10(total_ncs_tco2e),
    label   = if_else(
      (total_ncs_tco2e > quantile(total_ncs_tco2e, 0.85, na.rm = TRUE) &
       n_projects == 0) |
      n_projects > 20,
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PV.EST, y = log_ncs,
             color = income_level, size = n_projects + 0.5)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 15,
                  segment.color = "grey55", show.legend = FALSE,
                  segment.size = 0.3, box.padding = 0.35) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60") +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Market projects (n)", range = c(1, 8),
                        breaks = c(0, 5, 20, 50, 100, 200)) +
  labs(
    x = "Political Stability & Absence of Violence (WGI 2024)",
    y = "Total NCS Potential (log\u2081\u2080 tCO\u2082e yr\u207b\u00b9)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

panel_B
```

### 13.5 Two-panel paper figure

```{r two-panel-figure, fig.height=7, fig.width=18}
eq_counts <- read_csv(here("data/equator/equator_nbs_database.csv"),
                      show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3c)) %>%
  count(iso3c, name = "n_community_b")

df_b <- df_k %>%
  left_join(eq_counts, by = "iso3c") %>%
  mutate(
    n_community_b = replace_na(n_community_b, 0),
    n_all         = n_projects + n_community_b,
    ncs_MtCO2     = total_ncs_tco2e / 1e6,
    proj_per_pot  = n_all / pmax(ncs_MtCO2, 0.001),
    log_eff       = log1p(proj_per_pot),
    label = if_else(
      proj_per_pot > quantile(proj_per_pot, 0.88, na.rm = TRUE) |
      (proj_per_pot == 0 &
       ncs_MtCO2 > quantile(ncs_MtCO2, 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(PV.EST), ncs_MtCO2 > 0)

# ── Panel B: all projects per potential, colored by income ───────────────────
panel_B_paper <- df_b %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PV.EST, y = log_eff, color = income_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 2.0) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 2.3, max.overlaps = 12,
                  segment.size = 0.3, segment.color = "grey55",
                  box.padding = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60") +
  labs(
    x = "Political Stability & Absence of Violence (WGI 2024)",
    y = "log\u2081\u2080(Projects per MtCO\u2082e yr\u207b\u00b9 + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

# ── Panel C: faceted by Market vs Community ──────────────────────────────────
panel_C_paper <- df_b %>%
  transmute(
    iso3c, adm0_name, PV.EST, income_level, ncs_MtCO2,
    Market    = log1p(n_projects    / pmax(ncs_MtCO2, 0.001)),
    Community = log1p(n_community_b / pmax(ncs_MtCO2, 0.001))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(Market, Community),
               names_to = "Database", values_to = "log_eff") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PV.EST, y = log_eff, color = income_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60") +
  facet_wrap(~ Database, ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Political Stability & Absence of Violence (WGI 2024)",
    y = "log\u2081\u2080(Projects per MtCO\u2082e yr\u207b\u00b9 + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

# ── Combine all three ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig_paper <- (panel_A + theme(legend.position = "none")) +
  (panel_B_paper + theme(legend.position = "right")) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "A", tag_prefix = "(", tag_suffix = ")"
  )

fig_paper

dir.create(here("analysis/figures"), showWarnings = FALSE)
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig_paper_main.png"),
       fig_paper, width = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig_paper_main.pdf"),
       fig_paper, width = 14, height = 7)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig_paper_main.png (.pdf)\n")
```

### 13.6 Regression: Projects ~ NbS Potential + WGI

Do countries with higher governance implement more market-based NbS, *conditional*
on biophysical potential?

```{r karnik-reg, fig.height=5}
df_kreg <- df_k %>%
  filter(total_ncs_tco2e > 0, !is.na(income_level)) %>%
  mutate(
    ncs_MtCO2    = total_ncs_tco2e / 1e6,
    proj_per_pot = n_projects / pmax(ncs_MtCO2, 0.001),
    log_proj_pot = log1p(proj_per_pot)
  )

# OLS: projects per MtCO2e potential ~ WGI (no need to control for NCS size now)
m_ols <- lm(log_proj_pot ~ CC.EST + GE.EST + PV.EST +
              RL.EST + RQ.EST + VA.EST + income_level,
            data = df_kreg)

cat(sprintf("OLS adj. R² = %.3f  |  N = %d\n",
            summary(m_ols)$adj.r.squared, nobs(m_ols)))

tidy(m_ols, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("^(CC|GE|PV|RL|RQ|VA)", term)) %>%
  mutate(term = wgi_codes[term]) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high,
             y = reorder(term, estimate),
             color = p.value < 0.05)) +
  geom_pointrange(size = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#d73027", "FALSE" = "grey55"),
                     guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title    = "OLS: log1p(Market Projects per MtCO\u2082e/yr) ~ WGI",
    subtitle = sprintf("Projects per unit NbS potential  |  Adj. R\u00b2 = %.3f",
                       summary(m_ols)$adj.r.squared),
    x = "OLS Coefficient (95% CI)", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

---

## 14. Risk × Potential Quadrant Analysis

The central question of this paper: **do NbS projects cluster where we expect
them to, or does political risk systematically redirect implementation away from
high-potential countries?**

We place every country on two axes:

- **X — Political Risk** (inverted WGI Political Stability; higher = more risky)
- **Y — NbS Biophysical Potential** (log₁₀ total NCS tCO₂e/yr)

Quadrant boundaries are set at the **median** of each axis. Project activity
combines both datasets: **market-based projects** (Karnik 2024) and
**community-based projects** (Equator Initiative).

```{r load-equator}
eq_raw <- read_csv(here("data/equator/equator_nbs_database.csv"),
                   show_col_types = FALSE)

# Aggregate to country level (use only records with ISO3)
eq_cty <- eq_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3c)) %>%
  count(iso3c, name = "n_community")

cat(sprintf("Equator: %d countries, %d community projects with ISO3\n",
            nrow(eq_cty), sum(eq_cty$n_community)))
```

```{r quadrant-build}
# Build quadrant dataset
df_quad <- df_k %>%                         # already has Karnik joined
  left_join(eq_cty, by = "iso3c") %>%
  mutate(
    n_community   = replace_na(n_community, 0),
    n_total       = n_projects + n_community,   # market + community
    # Political risk = inverted stability (higher = more risky)
    risk          = -PV.EST,
    log_ncs       = log10(replace(total_ncs_tco2e, total_ncs_tco2e <= 0, NA)),
    # Quadrant thresholds at MEAN (more informative than median for skewed distributions)
    risk_med      = mean(risk,    na.rm = TRUE),
    ncs_med       = mean(log_ncs, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Assign quadrant
    quadrant = case_when(
      risk >= risk_med & log_ncs >= ncs_med ~ "High Risk\nHigh Potential",
      risk <  risk_med & log_ncs >= ncs_med ~ "Low Risk\nHigh Potential",
      risk >= risk_med & log_ncs <  ncs_med ~ "High Risk\nLow Potential",
      risk <  risk_med & log_ncs <  ncs_med ~ "Low Risk\nLow Potential",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    quadrant = factor(quadrant, levels = c(
      "Low Risk\nHigh Potential",  "High Risk\nHigh Potential",
      "Low Risk\nLow Potential",   "High Risk\nLow Potential"
    ))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(quadrant))

risk_med <- unique(df_quad$risk_med)
ncs_med  <- unique(df_quad$ncs_med)
```

### 14.1 Quadrant Scatter

```{r quadrant-scatter, fig.height=8, fig.width=11}
quad_fills <- c(
  "Low Risk\nHigh Potential"  = "#d9f0d3",   # green  — prime
  "High Risk\nHigh Potential" = "#fddbc7",   # orange — gap
  "Low Risk\nLow Potential"   = "#f7f7f7",   # grey   — limited
  "High Risk\nLow Potential"  = "#e8d5e8"    # purple — double constraint
)
quad_labels <- tibble(
  risk   = c(-2.2, 1.5, -2.2, 1.5),
  log_ncs = c(ncs_med + 1.3, ncs_med + 1.3,
              ncs_med - 1.0, ncs_med - 1.0),
  label  = c("LOW RISK\nHIGH POTENTIAL\n(Prime for implementation)",
             "HIGH RISK\nHIGH POTENTIAL\n(Implementation Gap)",
             "LOW RISK\nLOW POTENTIAL\n(Limited opportunity)",
             "HIGH RISK\nLOW POTENTIAL\n(Double constraint)"),
  hjust  = c(0, 1, 0, 1)
)

df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    pt_label = if_else(
      (n_total > 10) |
      (log_ncs > quantile(log_ncs, 0.88, na.rm = TRUE) & n_total == 0) |
      (risk > 1.5),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = risk, y = log_ncs)) +
  # Quadrant shading
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = risk_med,
           ymin = ncs_med,  ymax = Inf,  fill = quad_fills[1], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = risk_med, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = ncs_med,  ymax = Inf,  fill = quad_fills[2], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = risk_med,
           ymin = -Inf,    ymax = ncs_med, fill = quad_fills[3], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = risk_med, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = -Inf,    ymax = ncs_med, fill = quad_fills[4], alpha = 0.55) +
  # Median lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = risk_med, linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = ncs_med,  linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40") +
  # Points: size = total projects, fill = income
  geom_point(aes(size = n_total + 0.5, fill = income_level),
             shape = 21, alpha = 0.8, color = "white", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = pt_label), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 18,
                  segment.size = 0.3, segment.color = "grey50",
                  box.padding = 0.3) +
  # Quadrant text labels
  geom_text(data = quad_labels,
            aes(x = risk, y = log_ncs, label = label, hjust = hjust),
            vjust = 0.5, size = 2.8, color = "grey30",
            lineheight = 0.9, fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income", na.value = "grey70") +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Total projects\n(market + community)",
                        range = c(1.5, 9),
                        breaks = c(0, 10, 50, 100, 150)) +
  labs(
    title   = "Political Risk vs. NbS Potential: Where Are the Projects?",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Point size = market (Karnik 2024) + community (Equator Initiative) projects  |  ",
      "Dashed lines at medians"
    ),
    x       = "Political Risk  ←  Low         High  →\n(Inverted WGI Political Stability, 2024)",
    y       = "NbS Biophysical Potential (log₁₀ tCO₂e/yr)",
    caption = "Naturebase v3 (2025) · WGI 2024 · Karnik et al. 2024 · Equator Initiative 2024"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig3_quadrants.png"),
       last_plot(), width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig3_quadrants.pdf"),
       last_plot(), width = 11, height = 8)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig3_quadrants.png (.pdf)\n")
```

### 14.2 Quadrant Summary Table

```{r quadrant-table}
df_quad %>%
  group_by(quadrant) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries        = n(),
    total_ncs_GtCO2e   = sum(10^log_ncs, na.rm = TRUE) / 1e9,
    countries_w_market = sum(n_projects > 0),
    total_market_proj  = sum(n_projects),
    countries_w_comm   = sum(n_community > 0),
    total_comm_proj    = sum(n_community),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_market = round(countries_w_market / n_countries * 100),
    pct_comm   = round(countries_w_comm   / n_countries * 100)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Quadrant", "Countries", "NCS Potential (Gt/yr)",
                  "Countries w/ market project", "Market projects (n)",
                  "Countries w/ community project", "Community projects (n)",
                  "% w/ market", "% w/ community"),
    digits = 1,
    caption = "NbS Project Activity by Risk × Potential Quadrant"
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                            full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

### 14.3 Project Density by Quadrant

```{r quadrant-density, fig.height=4, fig.width=9}
df_quad %>%
  pivot_longer(c(n_projects, n_community),
               names_to = "type", values_to = "n") %>%
  mutate(type = recode(type,
    n_projects  = "Market (Karnik 2024)",
    n_community = "Community (Equator Initiative)"
  )) %>%
  group_by(quadrant, type) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(n), countries = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(per_country = total / countries) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = quadrant, y = per_country, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", per_country)),
            position = position_dodge(0.8), vjust = -0.4, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Market (Karnik 2024)" = "#1a9850",
                                "Community (Equator Initiative)" = "#2166ac"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("\n", " / ", x)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Average NbS Projects per Country by Quadrant",
    subtitle = "Market = carbon offset registries; Community = Equator Initiative",
    x = NULL, y = "Projects per country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

### 14.8 Area-normalised Scatter: NCS Density × Political Risk × Implementation

Both axes are normalised by country area, eliminating country-size as a
confounder entirely:

- **X** — Political Risk (inverted WGI Political Stability)
- **Y** — NCS potential density (tCO₂e per ha per year)
- **Size** — total projects per million ha (market + community)
- **Colour** — income group

```{r quadrant-area, fig.height=7, fig.width=10}
panel_C <- df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    area_mha      = adm0_ha / 1e6,
    ncs_per_ha    = total_ncs_tco2e / adm0_ha,
    total_per_mha = n_total / area_mha,
    log_ncs_ha    = log10(ncs_per_ha),
    label = if_else(
      (ncs_per_ha  > quantile(ncs_per_ha,    0.88, na.rm = TRUE) &
       total_per_mha == 0) |
      total_per_mha > quantile(total_per_mha, 0.88, na.rm = TRUE),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(log_ncs_ha), is.finite(log_ncs_ha)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = risk, y = log_ncs_ha)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = income_level, size = total_per_mha + 0.01),
             alpha = 0.78) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 18,
                  segment.color = "grey55", segment.size = 0.3,
                  box.padding = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60") +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "Projects / million ha\n(market + community)",
    range  = c(1, 9),
    breaks = c(0, 0.5, 2, 5, 10, 20),
    labels = c("0", "0.5", "2", "5", "10", "20")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Political Risk  \u2190 Low         High \u2192\n(Inverted WGI Political Stability, 2024)",
    y = "NCS Potential Density (log\u2081\u2080 tCO\u2082e ha\u207b\u00b9 yr\u207b\u00b9)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(order = 1, override.aes = list(size = 3)),
         size  = guide_legend(order = 2))

panel_C

# Save standalone
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig2_implementation.png"),
       panel_C, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig2_implementation.pdf"),
       panel_C, width = 10, height = 7)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig2_implementation.png (.pdf)\n")
```

```{r quadrant-area-table}
df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    area_mha      = adm0_ha / 1e6,
    ncs_per_ha    = total_ncs_tco2e / adm0_ha,
    total_per_mha = n_total / area_mha
  ) %>%
  group_by(quadrant) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries        = n(),
    mean_ncs_per_ha    = mean(ncs_per_ha,    na.rm = TRUE),
    median_ncs_per_ha  = median(ncs_per_ha,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_proj_per_mha  = mean(total_per_mha, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_proj_per_mha = median(total_per_mha, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Quadrant", "Countries",
                  "Mean NCS (tCO\u2082e/ha)", "Median NCS (tCO\u2082e/ha)",
                  "Mean projects/Mha", "Median projects/Mha"),
    caption = "Area-normalised NCS potential and project density by quadrant"
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

### 14.5 Threshold Analysis: 3×3 Risk × Potential Grid

If political risk operates as a **threshold effect** rather than a linear
gradient, we expect under-representation to concentrate in the highest-risk
tier only — not spread evenly across the high-risk half. Splitting both axes
into **tertiles** (low / medium / high) gives 9 cells and allows a direct test.

Colour in each cell = standardised residual $(O - E) / \sqrt{E}$ from a
chi-square test. Negative (red) = fewer projects than expected; positive
(green) = more. The annotation shows observed % of countries with ≥1 project.

```{r threshold-grid, fig.height=7, fig.width=9}
# Tertile breaks for both axes
df_grid <- df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    risk_tier = cut(risk,
      breaks = quantile(risk, c(0, 1/3, 2/3, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
      labels = c("Low Risk", "Medium Risk", "High Risk"),
      include.lowest = TRUE
    ),
    ncs_tier = cut(log_ncs,
      breaks = quantile(log_ncs, c(0, 1/3, 2/3, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
      labels = c("Low Potential", "Medium Potential", "High Potential"),
      include.lowest = TRUE
    ),
    has_project = n_total > 0
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(risk_tier), !is.na(ncs_tier))

# 3×3 contingency: countries WITH projects per cell
grid_tab <- df_grid %>%
  group_by(risk_tier, ncs_tier) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries    = n(),
    n_with_project = sum(has_project),
    pct            = round(mean(has_project) * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Chi-square on the 9-cell table (expected proportional to cell size)
total_with <- sum(grid_tab$n_with_project)
total_n    <- sum(grid_tab$n_countries)
grid_tab   <- grid_tab %>%
  mutate(
    expected = total_with * n_countries / total_n,
    std_resid = (n_with_project - expected) / sqrt(expected)
  )

chi9 <- chisq.test(
  matrix(grid_tab$n_with_project, nrow = 3,
         dimnames = list(levels(df_grid$risk_tier),
                         levels(df_grid$ncs_tier))),
  p = matrix(grid_tab$n_countries / total_n, nrow = 3),
  rescale.p = TRUE
)

cat(sprintf("3×3 chi-square: X²(%.0f) = %.2f, p = %.4f\n",
            chi9$parameter, chi9$statistic, chi9$p.value))

# Heatmap
grid_tab %>%
  mutate(
    risk_tier = factor(risk_tier, levels = rev(c("Low Risk","Medium Risk","High Risk"))),
    label     = sprintf("%d / %d\n(%g%%)", n_with_project, n_countries, pct)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ncs_tier, y = risk_tier, fill = std_resid)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 3.8, lineheight = 0.95) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#d73027", mid = "white", high = "#1a9850",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Std.\nresidual",
    limits   = c(-3, 3), oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "Project Presence Across 3×3 Risk × Potential Grid",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "Colour = standardised residual (O\u2212E)/\u221aE  |  Chi-square p = %.3f",
      chi9$p.value
    ),
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption  = paste("Tertile splits on both axes.",
                     "Karnik et al. (2024); Equator Initiative (2024);",
                     "Naturebase v3 (2025); WGI 2024")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r threshold-market-only, fig.height=7, fig.width=9}
# Repeat for MARKET projects only (Karnik) to separate from community projects
grid_mkt <- df_grid %>%
  mutate(has_market = n_projects > 0) %>%
  group_by(risk_tier, ncs_tier) %>%
  summarise(
    n_countries  = n(),
    n_with_mkt   = sum(has_market),
    pct          = round(mean(has_market) * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expected  = sum(n_with_mkt) * n_countries / sum(n_countries),
    std_resid = (n_with_mkt - expected) / sqrt(expected)
  )

chi_mkt <- chisq.test(
  matrix(grid_mkt$n_with_mkt, nrow = 3),
  p = matrix(grid_mkt$n_countries / sum(grid_mkt$n_countries), nrow = 3),
  rescale.p = TRUE
)
cat(sprintf("Market-only chi-square: X²(%.0f) = %.2f, p = %.4f\n",
            chi_mkt$parameter, chi_mkt$statistic, chi_mkt$p.value))
```

```{r mechanism-grids, fig.height=14, fig.width=14}
# Helper: build a 3×3 residual grid for any binary outcome column
make_grid <- function(data, outcome_col, label) {
  data %>%
    group_by(risk_tier, ncs_tier) %>%
    summarise(
      n_countries = n(),
      n_with      = sum(.data[[outcome_col]]),
      pct         = round(mean(.data[[outcome_col]]) * 100, 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      expected  = sum(n_with) * n_countries / sum(n_countries),
      std_resid = (n_with - expected) / sqrt(pmax(expected, 0.001)),
      mechanism = label,
      tile_label = sprintf("%d/%d\n(%g%%)", n_with, n_countries, pct)
    )
}

# Join sub-type project counts onto the grid df
# (n_ad, n_ifm, n_arr already present via df_quad → df_grid)
df_mech <- df_grid %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(n_ad, n_ifm, n_arr), ~ replace_na(., 0)),
    has_project   = n_total     > 0,
    has_market    = n_projects  > 0,
    has_community = n_community > 0,
    has_ad        = n_ad  > 0,
    has_ifm       = n_ifm > 0,
    has_arr       = n_arr > 0
  )

# Build one grid per mechanism
grids <- bind_rows(
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_project",   "All Projects\n(Market + Community)"),
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_market",    "Market (All)\nKarnik 2024"),
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_community", "Community\nEquator Initiative"),
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_ad",        "Avoided\nDeforestation (AD)"),
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_ifm",       "Improved Forest\nMgmt (IFM)"),
  make_grid(df_mech, "has_arr",       "Afforestation /\nReforestation (ARR)")
) %>%
  mutate(
    risk_tier = factor(risk_tier, levels = rev(c("Low Risk","Medium Risk","High Risk"))),
    mechanism = factor(mechanism, levels = unique(mechanism))
  )

# Chi-square p-values per mechanism
chi_pvals <- grids %>%
  group_by(mechanism) %>%
  summarise(
    p = tryCatch({
      chisq.test(n_with,
                 p = n_countries / sum(n_countries),
                 rescale.p = TRUE)$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(p_label = sprintf("p = %.3f", p))

grids <- left_join(grids, chi_pvals, by = "mechanism") %>%
  mutate(mechanism_label = paste0(as.character(mechanism), "\n", p_label))

ggplot(grids, aes(x = ncs_tier, y = risk_tier, fill = std_resid)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.0) +
  geom_text(aes(label = tile_label), size = 3.2, lineheight = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#d73027", mid = "white", high = "#1a9850",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Std.\nresidual",
    limits = c(-3, 3), oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  facet_wrap(~ mechanism_label, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title   = "Project Presence by Mechanism: 3×3 Risk × Potential Grid",
    subtitle = "Cell = countries with ≥1 project / total  |  Colour = (O−E)/√E",
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    caption = "Karnik et al. (2024); Equator Initiative (2024); Naturebase v3 (2025); WGI 2024"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    strip.text     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    panel.grid     = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig6_mechanism_grids.png"),
       last_plot(), width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig6_mechanism_grids.pdf"),
       last_plot(), width = 14, height = 10)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig6_mechanism_grids.png (.pdf)\n")
```

### 14.6 Political Risk × Project Implementation

Direct scatter of political risk against project counts, both raw and
area-normalised. These show whether high-risk countries implement fewer projects
independent of their NCS potential.

```{r risk-projects, fig.height=7, fig.width=14}
df_rp <- df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    area_mha      = adm0_ha / 1e6,
    total_per_mha = n_total / area_mha,
    label_raw = if_else(
      n_total > quantile(n_total, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE) |
      (risk > 1 & n_total > 5),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    ),
    label_dens = if_else(
      total_per_mha > quantile(total_per_mha, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE) |
      (risk > 1 & total_per_mha > 0.5),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  )

# Shared aesthetics
base_theme <- list(
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", name = "Income group",
                     na.value = "grey60"),
  theme_bw(base_size = 11),
  labs(x = "Political Risk\n(Inverted WGI Political Stability, 2024)")
)

p_raw <- df_rp %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = risk, y = log1p(n_total), color = income_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 2.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label_raw), size = 2.4, max.overlaps = 15,
                  segment.size = 0.3, segment.color = "grey55",
                  box.padding = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  base_theme +
  labs(y = "Total Projects (log\u2081\u2070(n + 1))\nMarket + Community")

p_dens <- df_rp %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = risk, y = log1p(total_per_mha), color = income_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 2.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
              linewidth = 0.8, fill = "grey85") +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label_dens), size = 2.4, max.overlaps = 15,
                  segment.size = 0.3, segment.color = "grey55",
                  box.padding = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  base_theme +
  labs(y = "Projects per Million ha (log\u2081\u2070(n + 1))\nMarket + Community")

fig_rp <- (p_raw + p_dens) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "A", tag_prefix = "(", tag_suffix = ")",
    caption = "Karnik et al. (2024); Equator Initiative (2024); WGI 2024"
  ) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

fig_rp

ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig4_risk_x_projects.png"),
       fig_rp, width = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig4_risk_x_projects.pdf"),
       fig_rp, width = 14, height = 7)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig4_risk_x_projects.png (.pdf)\n")
```

### 14.7 Implementation Efficiency: Projects per NCS Potential

**Projects per MtCO₂e of biophysical potential** — a direct measure of how
much of the available carbon opportunity is being activated. A country with
ten projects but 10 GtCO₂e of potential is implementing far less efficiently
than one with ten projects and 100 MtCO₂e of potential.

Two panels:
- **(A)** Projects per MtCO₂e/yr vs. political risk
- **(B)** Projects per (MtCO₂e/yr per Mha) — fully area-normalised efficiency

```{r proj-per-potential, fig.height=7, fig.width=9}
df_eff <- df_quad %>%
  mutate(
    ncs_MtCO2      = total_ncs_tco2e / 1e6,
    proj_per_MtCO2 = n_total / pmax(ncs_MtCO2, 0.001),
    log_eff        = log1p(proj_per_MtCO2)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(proj_per_MtCO2))

x_med <- mean(df_eff$risk,    na.rm = TRUE)
y_med <- mean(df_eff$log_eff, na.rm = TRUE)

quad_fills_eff <- c(
  "Low Risk\nHigh Efficiency"  = "#d9f0d3",
  "High Risk\nHigh Efficiency" = "#fddbc7",
  "Low Risk\nLow Efficiency"   = "#f7f7f7",
  "High Risk\nLow Efficiency"  = "#e8d5e8"
)

df_eff <- df_eff %>%
  mutate(
    quadrant_eff = case_when(
      risk <  x_med & log_eff >= y_med ~ "Low Risk\nHigh Efficiency",
      risk >= x_med & log_eff >= y_med ~ "High Risk\nHigh Efficiency",
      risk <  x_med & log_eff <  y_med ~ "Low Risk\nLow Efficiency",
      risk >= x_med & log_eff <  y_med ~ "High Risk\nLow Efficiency"
    ),
    label = if_else(
      proj_per_MtCO2 > quantile(proj_per_MtCO2, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE) |
      (n_total == 0 & ncs_MtCO2 > quantile(ncs_MtCO2, 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)),
      adm0_name, NA_character_
    )
  )

quad_text_eff <- tibble(
  risk    = c(-2.1,  1.4, -2.1,  1.4),
  log_eff = c(y_med + 0.8, y_med + 0.8,
              y_med - 0.5, y_med - 0.5),
  label   = c("Low Risk\nHigh Efficiency",
              "High Risk\nHigh Efficiency",
              "Low Risk\nLow Efficiency",
              "High Risk\nLow Efficiency"),
  hjust   = c(0, 1, 0, 1)
)

fig_eff <- df_eff %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = risk, y = log_eff)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = x_med,
           ymin = y_med, ymax = Inf,   fill = quad_fills_eff[1], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = x_med, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = y_med, ymax = Inf,   fill = quad_fills_eff[2], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = x_med,
           ymin = -Inf,  ymax = y_med, fill = quad_fills_eff[3], alpha = 0.55) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = x_med, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = -Inf,  ymax = y_med, fill = quad_fills_eff[4], alpha = 0.55) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = x_med, linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = y_med, linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(aes(fill = quadrant_eff), shape = 21, size = 2.4,
             alpha = 0.85, color = "white", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 2.4, max.overlaps = 15,
                  segment.size = 0.3, segment.color = "grey55",
                  box.padding = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = quad_text_eff,
            aes(label = label, hjust = hjust),
            vjust = 0.5, size = 2.7, color = "grey30",
            lineheight = 0.9, fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = quad_fills_eff, name = NULL,
                    guide = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
  labs(
    x       = "Political Risk  \u2190 Low         High \u2192\n(Inverted WGI Political Stability, 2024)",
    y       = "Implementation Efficiency\nlog\u2081\u2080(Projects per MtCO\u2082e yr\u207b\u00b9 + 1)",
    caption = "Karnik et al. (2024); Equator Initiative (2024); Naturebase v3 (2025); WGI 2024"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

fig_eff

ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig5_efficiency.png"),
       fig_eff, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig5_efficiency.pdf"),
       fig_eff, width = 9, height = 7)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig5_efficiency.png (.pdf)\n")
```

---

## 15. Risk Threshold × NbS Type: Cumulative Potential

As investors or policymakers lower their political risk tolerance, how much NbS
potential becomes inaccessible — and does this vary by ecosystem type?

We sort all countries from **most stable to most risky** (high → low PV.EST) and
trace how cumulative NbS potential grows as the acceptable risk threshold is
relaxed. The y-axis shows cumulative potential as a **share of the global total**
for each NbS type, so the four curves reach 100% at the same point (all countries
included) but at different rates — revealing which NbS types are most
disproportionately located in high-risk countries.

```{r risk-threshold, fig.height=6, fig.width=9}
# ── Helper: build a cumulative % curve for a named set of columns ─────────────
make_cumulative <- function(col_map) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(PV.EST)) %>%
    select(iso3c, PV.EST, all_of(names(col_map))) %>%
    arrange(desc(PV.EST)) %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(names(col_map)), ~ replace_na(., 0))) %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(names(col_map)),
                  ~ cumsum(.) / sum(.) * 100,
                  .names = "pct_{.col}")) %>%
    pivot_longer(starts_with("pct_"),
                 names_to = "type", values_to = "pct_cumulative") %>%
    mutate(type = col_map[sub("^pct_", "", type)])
}

biome_cols <- c(
  total_crop_tco2e   = "Cropland",
  total_forest_tco2e = "Forest",
  total_grass_tco2e  = "Grassland",
  total_wet_tco2e    = "Wetland"
)

activity_cols <- c(
  total_protect_tco2e = "Protection",
  total_manage_tco2e  = "Management",
  total_restore_tco2e = "Restoration"
)

df_biome    <- make_cumulative(biome_cols) %>%
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("Forest","Grassland","Cropland","Wetland")))
df_activity <- make_cumulative(activity_cols) %>%
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("Protection","Management","Restoration")))

# ── Shared plot helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
risk_zone_layers <- list(
  annotate("rect", xmin=-Inf, xmax=-1, ymin=0, ymax=100,
           fill="#fee0d2", alpha=0.35),
  annotate("rect", xmin=-1,   xmax=0,  ymin=0, ymax=100,
           fill="#fff7bc", alpha=0.35),
  annotate("rect", xmin=0,    xmax=1,  ymin=0, ymax=100,
           fill="#e5f5e0", alpha=0.25),
  annotate("rect", xmin=1,    xmax=Inf,ymin=0, ymax=100,
           fill="#a1d99b", alpha=0.25),
  geom_vline(xintercept=c(-1,0,1), linewidth=0.5,
             linetype="dashed", color="grey40"),
  annotate("text", x=c(-1.85,-0.5,0.5,1.85), y=97,
           label=c("Fragile","High Risk","Moderate","Stable"),
           size=2.9, color="grey35", fontface="italic"),
  scale_x_reverse(
    name   = "\u2190 Less Risky          More Risky \u2192\nPolitical Stability (WGI 2024, threshold)",
    breaks = seq(-3, 2, 1)
  ),
  scale_y_continuous(
    name   = "Cumulative NbS Potential (% of type total)",
    limits = c(0, 100),
    labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)
  ),
  theme_bw(base_size = 11),
  theme(legend.position = "right")
)

p_biome <- ggplot(df_biome,
                  aes(x = PV.EST, y = pct_cumulative, color = type)) +
  risk_zone_layers +
  # Activity types as grey lines (background layer)
  geom_line(data = df_activity,
            aes(x = PV.EST, y = pct_cumulative, linetype = type),
            color = "grey55", linewidth = 1.0, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # Biome types as coloured lines (foreground)
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Forest"    = "#1b7837", "Grassland" = "#78c679",
               "Cropland"  = "#d95f02", "Wetland"   = "#2166ac"),
    name = "Biome"
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = c("Protection"  = "solid",
               "Management"  = "longdash",
               "Restoration" = "dotted"),
    name = "Activity type"
  ) +
  guides(
    color    = guide_legend(order = 1, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.1)),
    linetype = guide_legend(order = 2,
                            keywidth = unit(2, "cm"),
                            override.aes = list(color = "grey55", linewidth = 1.0))
  )

p_biome
```

```{r risk-threshold-save}
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig_threshold_nbs_type.png"),
       last_plot(), width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave(here("analysis/figures/fig_threshold_nbs_type.pdf"),
       last_plot(), width = 9, height = 6)
cat("Saved: analysis/figures/fig_threshold_nbs_type.png (.pdf)\n")
```

---

## 16. Export Merged Dataset

```{r export}
export_cols <- c(
  "iso3c", "adm0_name", "adm0_ha",
  nbs_biome, nbs_activity, nbs_grand,
  wgi_vars, "income_level", "region",
  "n_projects", "n_ad", "n_ifm", "n_arr",
  "n_community", "n_total", "risk", "quadrant"
)

df_export <- df_quad %>%
  select(any_of(export_cols))

write_csv(df_export, here("data/nbs_wgi_merged.csv"))
cat("Exported", nrow(df_export), "countries →",
    here("data/nbs_wgi_merged.csv"), "\n")
```

---

## Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
